items.title: Declarative UI and re-rendering: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Declarative React means DOM events and state updates never occur imperatively.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Describe UI from state and use event handlers to update state; reserve DOM imperative work for explicit escape hatches.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Describe UI from state and use event handlers to update state; reserve DOM imperative work for explicit escape hatches.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Describe UI from state and use event handlers to update state; reserve DOM imperative work for explicit escape hatches.
Declarative React means DOM events and state updates never occur imperatively.

answer.explanation: Describe UI from state and use event handlers to update state; reserve DOM imperative work for explicit escape hatches.

items.title: Declarative UI and re-rendering: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Describe UI as a function of current props and state rather than imperative DOM mutations. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating UI derived from in-memory lesson data; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.

answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded react artifact for UI derived from in-memory lesson data. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
